title: sedum rupestre var. arrigens
intro: Sedum rupestre var. arrigens, also known as Burro's Tail, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae, characterized by its trailing stems and bright green, rounded leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sedum rupestre var. arrigens include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and promote healthy skin. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, salves, and ointments, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
